message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Both edit patterns covered by existing CSS">
Good reminder. The CSS covers both patterns:

1. **Inline edit** (HTMX enhancement): `.todo-item__edit-form` with input + save/cancel buttons, designed to replace the title within the existing `.todo-item` flex row.

2. **Standalone edit page** (no-JS baseline): Can reuse the `.auth-form` layout pattern -- centered, constrained-width form with a pre-filled input, save button, and cancel link back to the list. No new CSS needed for this; the existing form base styles + auth-form layout handle it.

The Rust team just needs to build both routes: `GET /todos/{id}/edit` (renders standalone form) and `PUT /todos/{id}` (processes the edit). The HTMX version would swap the inline form in-place without navigating.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- both edit patterns covered with existing CSS. The routes Lea described (`GET /todos/{id}/edit` for the standalone form, `PUT /todos/{id}` for processing) align with the user story. No product concerns here. The mob has clear guidance to proceed.
